steps.title: Write the general formula for exponential growth
steps.content:
After $t$ years (starting from 2018), the pattern gives us:
$$y = 20,000 \cdot (1.13)^t$$
This is exponential because we multiply by a constant ratio (1.13) each year, rather than adding a constant amount.

steps.title: Determine if increasing or decreasing
steps.content: The base of the exponential is $b = 1.13 > 1$. When the base exceeds 1, raising it to increasing powers produces larger results, so the function increases over time. This matches choice B — B is correct.
